title: Inconsistencies in Container Wait API documentation
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

The HTTP API reference for libpod/containers/{name}/wait at https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/ContainerWaitLibpod says that the output for `200` is content type `application/json` and of the form:
```
{
    "Error": 
    {
        "Message": "string"
    },
    "StatusCode": 0
}
```
However, when I try on an instance of podman 3.0.1, the reply is a single integer, which seems to me consistent with the code here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/382b5b81d24870abe400d14296e4f5ef47c99d29/pkg/api/handlers/utils/containers.go#L138

Is that possibly a bug in the documentation?

I also realized looking into the `WaitContainerLibpod` function, that there is  an undocumented parameter, `interval`. Should that possibly be documented?

comments.body:
I think other error codes could come back with strings.  IE If you attempt to wait on a non existent container.

Interval should be documented.

Thank you for your prompt reply!

> I think other error codes could come back with strings. IE If you attempt to wait on a non existent container.

That is definitely the case, but it seems to me that whenever that is the case, the response is either `404` or `500`:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/382b5b81d24870abe400d14296e4f5ef47c99d29/pkg/api/handlers/utils/containers.go#L129-L138

Is there actually a possibility that a `200` reply returns something else than a number? I understand that exitCode there is of type `entities.WaitReport.ExitCode`, just formatted as a string. Or am I missing something?

> Interval should be documented.

Ok, thank you!
No, I don't think the 200 would ever come back with a string.
